---
title: "Shape & Manifold Reconstruction"
subtitle: "Provable inference of manifold structure from finite samples."
image: ../images/plate-shape.svg
nocite: |
    @Fasy2022Recon, @Majhi2023, @Majhi2024, @majhi:LIPIcs.SoCG.2024.73, @Komendarczyk2024-su
---

Reconstructing a geometric shape&mdash;a curve, a manifold, a metric space&mdash;from finite, noisy samples is a foundational problem in topological data analysis. The work uses Vietoris&ndash;Rips and &Ccaron;ech complexes to thicken the sample at varying scales and asks: *when, and how faithfully, does the resulting complex remember the shape?*

Recent contributions:

- ***[Topological Stability and Latschev-type Reconstruction Theorems for CAT(κ) Spaces](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.04259)***&mdash;with Rafal Komendarczyk; extends Latschev's classical reconstruction theorem to spaces of bounded curvature.
- ***[The Shadow of Vietoris–Rips Complexes in Limits](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.01359)***&mdash;with Kazuhiro Kawamura and Atish Mitra.
- ***Demystifying Latschev's Theorem***&mdash;a quantitative version of the original reconstruction theorem, presented at SoCG&nbsp;2024.

## Collaborators
- Rafal Komendarczyk, Tulane University
- Atish Mitra, Montana Technological University
- Kazuhiro Kawamura, University of Tsukuba
- Brittany Fasy, Montana State University *(past)*
- Carola Wenk, Tulane University *(past)*

## Publications
::: {#refs}
:::
